_sources.fact: Burke County named after Thomas Burke, delegate to Continental Congress and governor (1781-82)
Morganton named in honor of General Daniel Morgan, Revolutionary War hero who led troops at Cowpens and King's Mountain; first chartered town in western NC in 1784
Morganton furniture manufacturing heritage: Henredon founded 1945, Drexel Furniture began 1903, Guy Chaddock relocated 2004. Historic courthouse square renovated and reopened May 4, 2022

localH2: Built for Morganton and the county it anchors.
localP1: Morganton has been Burke County's seat since 1784, when it was named in honor of General Daniel Morgan, the Revolutionary War hero who led troops at Cowpens and King's Mountain. For nearly 150 years, the town's backbone has been furniture making and manufacturing. Henredon, Drexel, and Guy Chaddock built operations here, turning local craftsmanship into brands that sold across America. That tradition of skilled work, attention to detail, and moving inventory at scale still runs through Morganton's economy.
localP2: We build for the people who keep this town going: the furniture maker running a shop downtown, the contractor and the machine operator, the restaurant and service business on Main Street. The foothills and the Catawba River draw steady traffic through Burke County, and your website needs to work as hard as you do. Every site is designed and coded around your business, not stamped out of a template.
mapMetaLine: Serving Morganton, Hickory, Conover, Newton, Rutherfordton & the foothills
